input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule F.6.3.4 specified in the FSAE rule document? To answer the question, use the scale bar shown at the top of the engineering drawing to compute necessary dimensions in the drawing.  First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.6.3.4
            TITLE: the front hoop braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the front hoop but

            CONTENT:
            rule F.6.3.4: The Front Hoop Braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the Front Hoop but
F.6.3.4 The Front Hoop Braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the Front Hoop but 
not more than 50 mm below the top-most surface of the Front Hoop.  See figure following

final_answer:
Explanation:Rule F.6.3.4 mandates that the Front Hoop Braces must be attached as near as possible to the top of the Front Hoop, but not more than 50 mm below the top-most surface. By examining the provided engineering drawing, particularly the front and side views, the attachment points for the Front Hoop Braces are visually positioned very close to the top curvature of the Front Hoop. The vertical distance between the top-most surface of the Front Hoop and the attachment points of the braces appears to be significantly less than the 50 mm maximum allowed distance. Therefore, the design appears to comply with rule F.6.3.4.
Answer:Yes
